title: FTSE 100 rallies as oil eases and Bank of England holds rates
body: Something went wrong The FTSE 100 made strong progress on Thursday as investors weighed a barrage of earnings, interest rate calls and the latest developments in the Middle East. The FTSE 100 closed up 165.71 points, or 1.6%, at 10,378.82. The FTSE 250 ended up 264.28 points, 1.2%, at 22,465.15, and the AIM All-Share rose 5.66 points, 0.7%, at 794.09. In London, the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee voted eight to one to leave bank rate on hold at 3.75%. Bank chief economist Huw Pill put the case for a quarter point rate increase to 4.0%. The Bank laid out three scenarios for the UK’s economic outlook as it assesses options for monetary policy amid the Middle East crisis and energy price shock.
